operation.title: Typed result measurement
operation.summary: Derive one finite scalar measurement from a bounded provenance-bound normalized real series using a closed deterministic algorithm.

assertion.statement: The declared measurement was derived, or was conclusively not observable, from the exact finite normalized source series using the declared unit-safe versioned algorithm.
non_goals: This assertion does not state that a design specification is satisfied.
This assertion does not validate the native EDA artifact parser that produced the normalized series.
This assertion does not interpolate complex values, convert units, infer operating conditions, or execute simulator expressions.
This assertion does not establish that the sampled domain or resolution is sufficient for signoff.

pass.meaning: A supported algorithm produced one finite scalar value with an explicit unit from a valid source series, and the result retains source, algorithm, and condition identity.
required_evidence: data.measurement.status is measured and data.measurement.value is finite.
data.measurement.unit is explicit and every coordinate or threshold unit exactly matched its declared axis or signal unit.
data.measurement.source retains producer request, artifact digest, normalized-series digest, and operating-condition bindings.
data.measurement.algorithm identifies the exact closed algorithm and version.

fail.meaning: The valid declared source domain conclusively contains no sample or event satisfying the requested closed measurement definition.
required_evidence: data.measurement.status is not_found and data.measurement.value is null.
The source series, units, algorithm parameters, and search domain were structurally valid.
A stable measurement.value.not_found diagnostic states which requested observation was absent.

unknown.meaning: The source or request is invalid, unsupported, unbounded, non-finite, unit-incompatible, or otherwise insufficient to justify a measured or not-found conclusion.
required_evidence: data.measurement.status is unknown and value is null.
At least one stable diagnostic identifies the invalid request, source, unit, domain, or algorithm condition.

parameters_schema.title: result.measure v1alpha1 request parameters
parameters_schema.description: A bounded normalized real series and exactly one closed measurement request.

semantic_constraints: Every JSON number must be finite; NaN and infinity are invalid even if a permissive parser accepts them.
The axis must contain 1 to 100000 strictly increasing values, every signal must have exactly the same length, and signal and condition names must be unique.
Every coordinate quantity must use the exact axis unit and every threshold or target quantity must use the exact selected-signal unit; v1alpha1 performs no unit conversion.
Windows are closed and must have stop greater than start. Reducers operate only on retained samples within the window.
Crossing and transition algorithms use deterministic linear interpolation only between adjacent samples and do not infer behavior between nonadjacent samples.
A settling-time request requires positive tolerance and hold_for; the signal must remain inside the closed tolerance band for at least hold_for in the observed domain.
series.source.artifact_sha256 must equal the implementation's canonical SHA-256 digest over the normalized axis, signals, and condition bindings; mismatches are invalid requests.
Any upstream native artifact may be retained only as optional lineage whose binding is explicitly unverified by this operation.

facts.description: Measured, not_found, or unknown classification without conflating absence with invalid evidence.
Canonical digest of the complete closed measurement definition used for the result.
Finite measured scalar for pass and null for fail or unknown.
Exact result unit inherited from the selected signal or axis.
Closed algorithm identity and semantic version.
Axis location associated with an extrema or event result when the algorithm defines one.
Producer request, artifact digest, normalized-series digest, and explicit conditions.

integrity: Bind the normalized series to one producer request ID and require its supplied artifact SHA-256 to equal the computed canonical content digest.
Retain any upstream native artifact only as optional unverified lineage; this operation does not claim to validate native waveform extraction.
Reject non-finite values, mismatched vector lengths, non-monotonic axes, duplicate names, and over-limit series before measuring.
artifact_roles.native_authority: The canonical normalized-series content is authoritative only for this measurement calculation. Optional native-artifact lineage is unverified and remains outside this assertion.

integrity_rules: Every output retains the exact normalized-series producer request and verified canonical content digest rather than treating copied numbers as anonymous evidence.
Measured and not-found outputs retain a canonical SHA-256 digest of the complete validated measurement request.
Unit equality is exact; v1alpha1 never guesses dimensions or silently rescales values.
A not-found event is fail only after the entire declared bounded domain is validly searched; malformed or insufficient evidence is unknown.
provenance_limitations: The operation does not validate that optional upstream native-artifact lineage was faithfully decoded into the normalized series.
Linear interpolation asserts only the versioned calculation between adjacent retained samples, not unobserved analog behavior.

features.request_effect: Return the selected signal at an in-domain axis coordinate by exact lookup or adjacent-sample linear interpolation.
features.conformance_case: Exact, interpolated, out-of-domain, and exact-sample-absent fixtures preserve pass/fail/unknown boundaries.

features.request_effect: Return the minimum retained signal sample and first matching axis location in an optional closed window.
features.conformance_case: Bounded fixtures cover ties, negative values, optional windows, and empty windows.

features.request_effect: Return the maximum retained signal sample and first matching axis location in an optional closed window.
features.conformance_case: Bounded fixtures cover ties, negative values, optional windows, and empty windows.

features.request_effect: Return the arithmetic mean of finite retained samples in an optional closed window using a stable summation primitive.
features.conformance_case: Fixtures cover positive and negative cancellation, windows, and empty windows.

features.request_effect: Return the root mean square of finite retained samples in an optional closed window.
features.conformance_case: Fixtures cover signed samples, windows, and empty windows.

features.request_effect: Return the selected rising, falling, or either-direction threshold crossing using adjacent-sample linear interpolation.
features.conformance_case: Fixtures cover direction, occurrence, plateaus, exact endpoints, windows, and absent crossings.

features.request_effect: Return elapsed axis distance from a rising lower-threshold crossing to its matching upper-threshold crossing.
features.conformance_case: Fixtures cover multiple pulses, incomplete transitions, ordering, and unit mismatch.

features.request_effect: Return elapsed axis distance from a falling upper-threshold crossing to its matching lower-threshold crossing.
features.conformance_case: Fixtures cover multiple pulses, incomplete transitions, ordering, and unit mismatch.

features.request_effect: Return elapsed axis distance from an explicit reference until all retained samples remain inside a closed target tolerance band for the declared hold duration.
features.conformance_case: Fixtures cover excursions, insufficient hold duration, boundary equality, windows, and absent settling.

diagnostics.condition: The closed request shape, identifier, number, parameter, or semantic constraint is invalid.
diagnostics.next_action: Correct the explicit typed request; do not invoke or infer a different algorithm.

diagnostics.condition: The normalized series source identity, shape, axis, signal, condition, or value is invalid.
diagnostics.next_action: Regenerate the bounded normalized series from validated upstream evidence.

diagnostics.condition: The supplied normalized-series artifact digest does not equal the canonical digest of the axis, signals, and conditions.
diagnostics.next_action: Reject the source as tampered or misbound and regenerate its canonical digest; do not relabel an unrelated native artifact hash.

diagnostics.condition: The inline series exceeds a profile point, signal, or condition ceiling.
diagnostics.next_action: Select or decimate evidence through an explicitly versioned upstream operation; do not truncate silently.

diagnostics.condition: The request names an algorithm outside the closed v1alpha1 vocabulary.
diagnostics.next_action: Choose an advertised feature or introduce a separately versioned algorithm profile.

diagnostics.condition: The named signal is absent from the normalized source series.
diagnostics.next_action: Correct the exact signal binding or regenerate the source with that signal.

diagnostics.condition: A coordinate or threshold unit does not exactly match the declared axis or signal unit.
diagnostics.next_action: Supply exact matching units; v1alpha1 performs no implicit conversion.

diagnostics.condition: A coordinate or reference lies outside the source domain.
diagnostics.next_action: Correct the requested domain or produce evidence spanning the required coordinate.

diagnostics.condition: A valid bounded source contains no sample or event satisfying the requested measurement.
diagnostics.next_action: Review the domain, resolution, direction, thresholds, and observation duration before rerunning.

diagnostics.condition: The algorithm did not produce one finite scalar despite structurally valid inputs.
diagnostics.next_action: Inspect numeric scale and algorithm inputs; retain unknown rather than emitting NaN or infinity.

semantic_bindings.implementation: Exact lookup or adjacent-point linear interpolation, algorithm version 1.0.0.
First minimum retained sample in the closed selected domain, algorithm version 1.0.0.
First maximum retained sample in the closed selected domain, algorithm version 1.0.0.
Arithmetic mean using finite stable summation over retained samples, algorithm version 1.0.0.
Square root of the arithmetic mean of squared finite retained samples, algorithm version 1.0.0.
Directed adjacent-point threshold crossing with linear axis interpolation, algorithm version 1.0.0.
Matched rising lower-to-upper crossing duration, algorithm version 1.0.0.
Matched falling upper-to-lower crossing duration, algorithm version 1.0.0.
First retained sample whose suffix remains within tolerance for the explicit hold duration, algorithm version 1.0.0.

native_actions: Validate and normalize the closed inline series without executing an EDA or user-provided expression.
Bind all coordinates and thresholds by exact unit equality and select exactly one named signal.
Execute one versioned deterministic scalar algorithm and reject non-finite results.
Retain source artifact, producer request, normalized content digest, operating conditions, and algorithm identity in the result.
limitations: The v1alpha1 kernel accepts only real finite series; upstream operations must explicitly derive magnitude, phase, or other real views from complex native evidence.
No arbitrary expressions, unit conversion, spline interpolation, resampling, FFT, statistical inference, or native simulator measurement language is accepted.

normative_surfaces: operation and assertion identifiers and pass/fail/unknown truth table
closed normalized-series and measurement request shapes
exact unit, domain, source-identity, and finite-number constraints
algorithm feature identifiers, parameter meanings, interpolation rules, and implementation version
normalized measurement record, request digest, source digest chain, and diagnostic meanings
compatibility.change_policy: Any change to an algorithm, parameter, unit rule, event boundary, source requirement, status classification, or normalized fact requires a new operation or feature identifier. Implementation corrections that preserve the published semantics require a new implementation version and conformance record.
